extraction.one_line_summary: BOASSON HAGEN Edvald attacked at 3km on a roundabout right-turn line choice, accelerated off the front with superior power ceiling that breakaway couldn't match despite all marking him as fastest finisher.
tactical_patterns: Boasson Hagen's roundabout geometry advantage at 3km eliminated braking/reacceleration penalty, gaining 50-meter gap before power application.
Breakaway completely paralyzed by single-rider marking problem: all knew Boasson Hagen fastest, attacks designed to avoid sprint still closed by him alone.
Sky isolated Froome with 4-minute deficit before final, signaling GC rivals conceded time trial advantages exceeding 45-60 seconds per commentary.

rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: closed every breakaway attack solo without teammates, revealing power ceiling sustainable in 3km+ efforts unlike pure sprinters
rider_signals.ARNDT Nicholas: negative: couldn't hold Boasson Hagen wheel despite being second-fastest sprinter in break, suggesting acceleration deficit not top-end speed
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: GC competitors gave 23-second lead into time trial despite historically smallest margin, revealing perceived 45-60 second chrono dominance
